data.title: Traders Anticipate Two Fed Rate Cuts by Year-End After Powell's Speech
data.description: According to BlockBeats, market reports indicate that following a speech by U.S. Federal Reserve Chair Jerome Powell, traders have fully resumed expectations for two interest rate cuts by the Federal Reserve before the end of the year.

data.title: Dollar Bullish Sentiment Peaks as Traders Adjust Fed Rate Cut Bets
data.description:
According to BlockBeats, foreign exchange options indicate that bullish sentiment on the dollar has reached a three-week high. This suggests that traders are betting U.S. Federal Reserve Chair Jerome Powell will not adopt an overly dovish stance on rate cuts.

Recently, traders have been reducing their bets on rate cuts. The market now anticipates a cumulative rate cut of 47 basis points by the end of the year, down from 63 basis points just over a week ago.

Sonja Marten, Head of FX and Monetary Policy Research at DZ Bank AG, stated in an interview with Bloomberg Television, "The door for a 25 basis point rate cut in September is open, but I don't think he will take a more aggressive stance than that."

Additionally, according to CME's FedWatch tool, there is currently a 24.5% probability that the Federal Reserve will keep rates unchanged in September, with a 75.5% chance of a 25 basis point cut. For October, the probability of maintaining rates is 13%, with a 51.5% chance of a cumulative 25 basis point cut and a 35.5% chance of a cumulative 50 basis point cut.

data.title: Fed Chair Powell Highlights Temporary Tariff Effects and Economic Framework
data.description: According to PANews, Federal Reserve Chair Jerome Powell stated that the effects of tariffs on prices are temporary and considered a reasonable baseline scenario. He noted that the stable unemployment rate allows the Federal Reserve to be more cautious when adjusting its policy stance. Powell emphasized the importance of preventing one-time price increases from evolving into persistent inflation issues and introduced a new framework to accommodate various economic conditions. Additionally, Powell highlighted the downside risks facing the employment situation. Following his remarks, traders increased the probability of a Federal Reserve rate cut in September from 75% to approximately 90%.

data.title: Why Jerome Powell Speech At Jackson Hole Could Make or Mar Crypto Market
data.description:

Notably, both equities and digital assets are bracing for sharp movements in the aftermath of the speech

data.title: BNB Chain's Q2 2025 Growth Highlights Record User Activity and Transactions
data.description:
According to BlockBeats, the latest report from Messari on BNB Chain for Q2 2025 reveals continued robust growth, with user activity and on-chain transactions reaching new highs. Daily active addresses and transaction volumes saw significant increases, while DeFi activity remained strong. BNB Chain led in DEX trading volume and stablecoin transactions, reinforcing its position as a leader in the Web3 ecosystem.

Key highlights include a 7.5% increase in BNB's market capitalization, reaching $92.6 billion, reflecting investor confidence. On-chain transactions surged by 101.9% to an average of 9.9 million daily, and daily active addresses grew by 33.2% to 1.6 million, with 17 million new addresses added in May.

In the DEX sector, BNB Chain maintained the highest trading volume across all chains, with a Q2 daily average of $3.3 billion. PancakeSwap held an 85.1% market share, while Uniswap's volume increased by 755.4% to $297 million. Network fees decreased by 90% to 0.1 gwei.

Stablecoins saw a market cap increase of 49.6% to $10.5 billion, with USD1 debuting at $2.2 billion. USDT dominated with a market cap of $6.3 billion. BNB Chain led in stablecoin transaction numbers and active users.

DeFi's total value locked (TVL) rose by 14% to $9.9 billion, securing the fourth position overall. Technical upgrades included the Lorentz and Maxwell hard forks, reducing block time to 0.75 seconds. The opBNB and Volta upgrades increased cross-chain TVL to $64.9 million, with Volta achieving sub-second finality by reducing block intervals to 500 milliseconds.

Ecosystem support was evident with the ninth season of MVB backing 16 early-stage projects. Additionally, a stablecoin zero-fee carnival subsidized $4 million in gas fees to accelerate user adoption.
